---
title: "Education"
description: "Educational attainment and the cost of higher education in Canada"
---

Education shapes both individual opportunity and the wider economy. This page looks at two questions: how educated Canadians are — by international standards, among the most credentialed populations in the OECD — and what a university education costs. (Tuition is reported by province and nationally; Statistics Canada does not publish fees for individual institutions.)

```{python}
#| echo: false
import os
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from pipeline.config import CANADA_COLOR, CATEGORICAL_COLORS, get_data_date
from pipeline.charts import lines_over_time, category_bar, province_colors, stacked_area

# Load tuition and deflate to constant dollars of the latest year, using the
# all-items CPI (annual average) — so the trend charts show *real* tuition.
tuition_path = "data/education/statcan_tuition.csv"
tu = None
if os.path.exists(tuition_path):
    tu = pd.read_csv(tuition_path)
    cpi = pd.read_csv("data/economics/statcan_cpi.csv", parse_dates=["date"])
    if "product_group" in cpi.columns:
        cpi = cpi[cpi["product_group"] == "All-items"]
    cpi_annual = cpi.assign(yr=cpi["date"].dt.year).groupby("yr")["cpi_value"].mean()
    base_year = int(tu["year"].max())
    tu["real"] = [row.tuition * cpi_annual[base_year] / cpi_annual[row.year]
                  if row.year in cpi_annual.index else float("nan")
                  for row in tu.itertuples()]
```

## How educated is Canada?

By international standards Canada has one of the most highly educated populations in the world. About **64%** of Canadians aged 25–64 have completed some form of **tertiary education** — university, college or CEGEP — well above the **OECD average of roughly 41%**, a lead that has held and widened since 2000. Younger Canadians are more educated still: about **68%** of 25-to-34-year-olds hold a tertiary credential. Much of Canada's standing reflects its large college and CEGEP system, not universities alone.

```{python}
#| echo: false
ta_path = "data/education/statcan_tertiary_attainment.csv"
if os.path.exists(ta_path):
    ta = pd.read_csv(ta_path)
    t = ta[(ta["age_group"] == "25–64 (all)") & (ta["level"] == "Tertiary (all)")
           & (ta["gender"] == "Total")].copy()
    t["date"] = pd.to_datetime(t["year"], format="%Y")
    ca = t[t["geo"] == "Canada"].sort_values("date")
    oe = t[t["geo"] == "OECD average"].sort_values("date")
    fig = go.Figure()
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=oe["date"], y=oe["pct"], name="OECD average", mode="lines",
        line=dict(color="#555555", width=2, dash="dash"), hovertemplate="OECD average: %{y:.0f}%<extra></extra>"))
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=ca["date"], y=ca["pct"], name="Canada", mode="lines+markers",
        line=dict(color=CANADA_COLOR, width=3), marker=dict(size=5), hovertemplate="Canada: %{y:.0f}%<extra></extra>"))
    fig.update_layout(plot_bgcolor="white", height=480, hovermode="x unified",
        yaxis=dict(title="Adults 25–64 with tertiary education (%)", ticksuffix="%", gridcolor="#e0e0e0", rangemode="tozero"),
        xaxis=dict(title="", gridcolor="#e0e0e0", type="date", rangeslider=dict(visible=True)),
        legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, x=0, bgcolor="rgba(255,255,255,0.6)"),
        margin=dict(t=40, b=70, r=20))
    fig.add_annotation(text=f"Source: Statistics Canada, Table 37-10-0130-01 | Data as of: {get_data_date(ta_path)}",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.34, xanchor="left", showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Attainment data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

### What kind of tertiary education?

"Tertiary" combines two quite different paths: shorter, more applied **college and CEGEP** programs (short-cycle tertiary) and **university** degrees. Splitting Canada's 25–64 figure into its parts shows how much of the country's high attainment rests on the college and CEGEP system — roughly as large a share as bachelor's degrees — alongside a steadily growing share holding master's or doctoral qualifications.

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(ta_path):
    br = ta[(ta["age_group"] == "25–64 (all)") & (ta["geo"] == "Canada")
            & (ta["gender"] == "Total") & (ta["level"] != "Tertiary (all)")].copy()
    # Attainment counts the HIGHEST credential, so the bachelor's band excludes
    # those who went on to a master's/doctorate — label it "(only)" to avoid the
    # "bachelor's or higher" misread.
    br["level"] = br["level"].replace({"Bachelor's": "Bachelor's (only)"})
    order = ["College/CEGEP", "Bachelor's (only)", "Master's/Doctoral"]
    fig = stacked_area(br, "year", "pct", "level",
        yaxis_title="Adults 25–64 (%)", group_order=order,
        colors={"College/CEGEP": CATEGORICAL_COLORS[0], "Bachelor's (only)": CATEGORICAL_COLORS[2],
                "Master's/Doctoral": CATEGORICAL_COLORS[3]},
        yticksuffix="%", hover_decimals=0, rangeslider=True,
        source_note=f"Source: Statistics Canada, Table 37-10-0130-01 | Data as of: {get_data_date(ta_path)}")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Attainment data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

### The gender gap

Among working-age Canadians, women are now more likely than men to hold a tertiary credential, and the gap has widened over time — from about five percentage points in 2000 to roughly fourteen today. The lead shows up at every level. At the master's and doctoral level, where men were slightly ahead at the start of the century, women have since moved in front. Use the dropdown to switch between any tertiary education and its components.

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(ta_path):
    g = ta[(ta["age_group"] == "25–64 (all)") & (ta["geo"] == "Canada")
           & (ta["gender"].isin(["Men", "Women"]))].copy()
    g["date"] = pd.to_datetime(g["year"], format="%Y")
    levels = ["Tertiary (all)", "College/CEGEP", "Bachelor's", "Master's/Doctoral"]
    level_labels = {"Tertiary (all)": "Any tertiary", "College/CEGEP": "College / CEGEP",
                    "Bachelor's": "Bachelor's (only)", "Master's/Doctoral": "Master's / doctoral"}
    sexes = [("Men", "#4477aa"), ("Women", "#ee8866")]
    default = "Tertiary (all)"
    fig = go.Figure()
    for sex, color in sexes:
        s = g[(g["gender"] == sex) & (g["level"] == default)].sort_values("date")
        fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=s["date"], y=s["pct"], name=sex, mode="lines+markers",
            line=dict(color=color, width=3), marker=dict(size=5),
            hovertemplate=f"{sex}: %{{y:.0f}}%<extra></extra>"))
    buttons = []
    for lvl in levels:
        xs, ys = [], []
        for sex, _ in sexes:
            s = g[(g["gender"] == sex) & (g["level"] == lvl)].sort_values("date")
            xs.append(s["date"].tolist()); ys.append(s["pct"].tolist())
        buttons.append(dict(label=level_labels[lvl], method="restyle", args=[{"x": xs, "y": ys}]))
    fig.update_layout(plot_bgcolor="white", height=480, hovermode="x unified",
        yaxis=dict(title="Adults 25–64 (%)", ticksuffix="%", gridcolor="#e0e0e0", rangemode="tozero"),
        xaxis=dict(title="", gridcolor="#e0e0e0", type="date", rangeslider=dict(visible=True)),
        legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, x=0, bgcolor="rgba(255,255,255,0.6)"),
        updatemenus=[dict(buttons=buttons, active=0, showactive=True,
            x=1.0, xanchor="right", y=1.14, yanchor="top")],
        margin=dict(t=66, b=70, r=20))
    fig.add_annotation(text="Attainment level:", xref="paper", yref="paper",
        x=0.60, y=1.15, xanchor="right", yanchor="top", showarrow=False, font=dict(size=11, color="#555"))
    fig.add_annotation(text=f"Source: Statistics Canada, Table 37-10-0130-01 | Data as of: {get_data_date(ta_path)}",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.34, xanchor="left", showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Attainment data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

### Gender balance across fields of study

Graduates are not spread evenly across disciplines. Women earn the majority of credentials in most fields — and a large majority in education, health, and the social sciences — while men remain the majority in only two: mathematics and computer science, and architecture and engineering. The first chart shows the split among the most recent year's graduates; the second shows how each field's balance has moved since 1992. Most fields have been broadly stable, but the share of women among computer-science graduates is lower today than it was in the early 1990s, while engineering has edged up from a low base.

```{python}
#| echo: false
gf_path = "data/education/statcan_grads_by_field.csv"
if os.path.exists(gf_path):
    gf = pd.read_csv(gf_path)
    yr = int(gf["year"].max())
    latest = gf[gf["year"] == yr]
    levels = ["All credentials", "College", "Bachelor's", "Master's", "Doctorate"]
    # Fixed field order by the overall (all-credentials) women share — highest at
    # top — kept constant so the dropdown only restyles values, not the row order.
    fields = list(latest[latest["level"] == "All credentials"].sort_values("pct_women")["field"])
    def _xy(lvl):
        s = latest[latest["level"] == lvl].set_index("field").reindex(fields)
        w = s["pct_women"].tolist()
        return (w, [100 - v if v == v else None for v in w],
                s["women"].tolist(), s["men"].tolist())
    w0, m0, wc0, mc0 = _xy("All credentials")
    fig = go.Figure()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(y=fields, x=w0, name="Women", orientation="h", marker_color="#ee8866",
        customdata=wc0, text=[f"{v:.0f}%" if v == v else "" for v in w0],
        textposition="inside", insidetextanchor="middle", textfont=dict(color="white", size=11),
        hovertemplate="Women: %{x:.1f}%  (%{customdata:,.0f} graduates)<extra></extra>"))
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(y=fields, x=m0, name="Men", orientation="h", marker_color="#4477aa",
        customdata=mc0, hovertemplate="Men: %{x:.1f}%  (%{customdata:,.0f} graduates)<extra></extra>"))
    buttons = []
    for lvl in levels:
        w, m, wc, mc = _xy(lvl)
        buttons.append(dict(label=lvl, method="restyle", args=[{
            "x": [w, m], "customdata": [wc, mc],
            "text": [[f"{v:.0f}%" if v == v else "" for v in w], [""] * len(m)]}]))
    fig.add_vline(x=50, line_dash="dash", line_color="#333", line_width=1.5)
    fig.update_layout(barmode="stack", plot_bgcolor="white", height=480,
        xaxis=dict(title="Share of graduates (%)", ticksuffix="%", range=[0, 100], gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
        yaxis=dict(title="", automargin=True),
        legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, x=0, bgcolor="rgba(255,255,255,0.6)"),
        updatemenus=[dict(buttons=buttons, active=0, showactive=True,
            x=1.0, xanchor="right", y=1.14, yanchor="top")],
        margin=dict(t=72, b=104, r=20))
    fig.add_annotation(text="Credential level:", xref="paper", yref="paper",
        x=0.66, y=1.15, xanchor="right", yanchor="top", showarrow=False, font=dict(size=11, color="#555"))
    fig.add_annotation(text="50% = equal balance", x=50, y=1.0, yref="paper", yanchor="bottom",
        showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#666"))
    fig.add_annotation(text=f"Source: Statistics Canada, Table 37-10-0135-01 (graduates, {yr}) | Data as of: {get_data_date(gf_path)}",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.20, xanchor="left", showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))
    fig.show(config={"displayModeBar": False})
else:
    print("Field-of-study data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(gf_path):
    tot = gf[gf["level"] == "All credentials"]
    order = list(tot[tot["year"] == yr].sort_values("pct_women", ascending=False)["field"])
    key = ["Education", "Health", "Social sciences & law", "Humanities",
           "Physical & life sciences", "Business & public admin",
           "Math & computer science", "Architecture & engineering"]
    legendonly = [f for f in order if f not in key]
    fig = lines_over_time(tot, "year", "pct_women", "field",
        yaxis_title="Women (% of graduates)", yticksuffix="%", hover_decimals=1,
        group_order=order, legendonly_groups=legendonly,
        source_note=f"Source: Statistics Canada, Table 37-10-0135-01 | Data as of: {get_data_date(gf_path)}")
    fig.add_hline(y=50, line_dash="dash", line_color="#999", line_width=1,
        annotation_text="Parity (50%)", annotation_position="bottom right",
        annotation_font=dict(size=10, color="#777"))
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Field-of-study data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Undergraduate tuition

Average undergraduate tuition for Canadian students, for Canada and the three provinces home to most of the country's largest universities. Quebec (and Newfoundland) hold tuition well below the national average; Ontario sits above it. Use the dropdown to switch between **real** (inflation-adjusted, constant dollars) and **nominal** (current dollars) — real terms isolate increases beyond general inflation.

```{python}
#| echo: false
if tu is not None:
    ug = tu[(tu["level"] == "Canadian undergraduate")
            & (tu["geography"].isin(["Canada", "Ontario", "Quebec", "British Columbia",
                                     "Alberta", "Nova Scotia", "Newfoundland and Labrador"]))].dropna(subset=["real"]).copy()
    ug["geography"] = ug["geography"].replace({"Newfoundland and Labrador": "Newfoundland<br>and Labrador"})
    fig = lines_over_time(ug, "year", "real", "geography",
        yaxis_title=f"Real tuition (constant {base_year} $)", ytickprefix="$", hover_decimals=0,
        colors=province_colors(["Canada", "Ontario", "Quebec", "British Columbia",
                                "Alberta", "Nova Scotia", "Newfoundland<br>and Labrador"]),
        group_order=["Canada", "Ontario", "Quebec", "British Columbia",
                     "Alberta", "Nova Scotia", "Newfoundland<br>and Labrador"],
        legendonly_groups=["Alberta", "Nova Scotia", "Newfoundland<br>and Labrador"], x_pad=True,
        measures=[{"col": "real", "label": f"Real (constant {base_year} $)", "yaxis_title": f"Real tuition (constant {base_year} $)"},
                  {"col": "tuition", "label": "Nominal (current $)", "yaxis_title": "Tuition (current $)"}],
        source_note=f"Source: Statistics Canada 37-10-0045-01, deflated by CPI (18-10-0004-01) | Data as of: {get_data_date(tuition_path)}")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Tuition data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Domestic vs. international tuition

International students pay several times what domestic students do, and that gap has widened sharply — international undergraduate fees have far outpaced inflation, becoming a significant revenue source for universities. Use the dropdown to switch between real (constant) and nominal (current) dollars (Canada-wide).

```{python}
#| echo: false
if tu is not None:
    ca = tu[tu["geography"] == "Canada"].dropna(subset=["real"]).copy()
    ca["level"] = ca["level"].str.replace(" ", "<br>", regex=False)   # two-line legend entries
    fig = lines_over_time(ca, "year", "real", "level",
        yaxis_title=f"Real tuition (constant {base_year} $)", ytickprefix="$", hover_decimals=0,
        colors={"International<br>undergraduate": CATEGORICAL_COLORS[5], "International<br>graduate": CATEGORICAL_COLORS[6],
                "Canadian<br>undergraduate": CATEGORICAL_COLORS[0], "Canadian<br>graduate": CATEGORICAL_COLORS[2]},
        group_order=["International<br>undergraduate", "International<br>graduate",
                     "Canadian<br>undergraduate", "Canadian<br>graduate"], x_pad=True,
        measures=[{"col": "real", "label": f"Real (constant {base_year} $)", "yaxis_title": f"Real tuition (constant {base_year} $)"},
                  {"col": "tuition", "label": "Nominal (current $)", "yaxis_title": "Tuition (current $)"}],
        source_note=f"Source: Statistics Canada 37-10-0045-01, deflated by CPI (18-10-0004-01) | Data as of: {get_data_date(tuition_path)}")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
```

## Undergraduate tuition by field of study

What students pay varies widely by program. Professional fields — dentistry, medicine, law, pharmacy — sit far above the average, while education and the humanities are lowest. (Canada-wide, Canadian undergraduate students, latest year.)

```{python}
#| echo: false
field_path = "data/education/statcan_tuition_by_field.csv"
if os.path.exists(field_path):
    bf = pd.read_csv(field_path)
    bf = bf[(bf["geography"] == "Canada") & (bf["field"] != "Total, field of study")]
    latest = int(bf["year"].max())
    bf = bf[bf["year"] == latest].copy()
    bf["field"] = bf["field"].replace({
        "Business, management and public administration": "Business and public admin",
        "Social and behavioural sciences, and legal studies": "Social sciences",
        "Mathematics, computer and information sciences": "Math and computer sciences",
        "Physical and life sciences and technologies": "Physical & life sciences",
        "Visual and performing arts, and communications technologies": "Visual & performing arts",
        "Agriculture, natural resources and conservation": "Agriculture & natural resources",
        "Personal, protective and transportation services": "Personal & protective services",
        "Other health, parks, recreation and fitness": "Health, parks & recreation",
    })
    fig = category_bar(bf, "field", "tuition",
        xaxis_title=f"Average undergraduate tuition, {latest}/{latest + 1} ($)",
        tickprefix="$", value_fmt=",.0f",
        source_note=f"Source: Statistics Canada 37-10-0003-01 | Data as of: {get_data_date(field_path)}")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Tuition-by-field data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Download data

- [Tertiary educational attainment, Canada vs OECD (CSV)](../data/education/statcan_tertiary_attainment.csv) — Statistics Canada 37-10-0130-01
- [Postsecondary graduates by field of study & gender (CSV)](../data/education/statcan_grads_by_field.csv) — Statistics Canada 37-10-0135-01
- [University tuition by level & province (CSV)](../data/education/statcan_tuition.csv) — Statistics Canada 37-10-0045-01
- [Undergraduate tuition by field of study (CSV)](../data/education/statcan_tuition_by_field.csv) — Statistics Canada 37-10-0003-01
